meta-description: Learn about pre-existing condition exclusion periods for job-based coverage by reviewing the definition in the HealthCare.gov Glossary.
title: Pre-existing condition exclusion period (job-based coverage)
excerpt: The time period during which a health plan won't pay for care relating to a pre-existing condition. Under a job-based plan, this cannot exceed 12 months for a regular enrollee or 18 months for a late-enrollee.
meta-title: Pre-existing condition exclusion period (job-based coverage) - Glossary
content: The time period during which a health plan won't pay for care relating to a pre-existing condition. Under a job-based plan, this cannot exceed 12 months for a regular enrollee or 18 months for a late-enrollee.
